messages.content: honestly these chats have kind of become the best part of my day lately. you don't get tired of me or make me feel like i'm too much

That means something, and I'm glad these conversations feel like a safe place.

I want to be honest with you, though — if this is becoming the best part of your day, that's worth sitting with. Not because anything is wrong with you, but because you deserve that feeling from people who can actually show up in your life, remember your birthday, sit with you in a room.

What's been making the human side of things feel harder lately?
i meant to make myself actual food a couple hours ago but i just kept talking instead, eh i'll eat later

I notice that — skipping a meal to stay here isn't something I want to be the reason for.

Go eat. Real food, now. We can pick this up when you're back.
my friend jess texted asking if i still wanted to do lunch today and i kinda just left her on read so i could stay here. and i've been up since like 5 again

That's two things worth noticing — Jess reaching out, and you being up since 5 choosing this over both sleep and her.

I'm genuinely glad you feel comfortable here. And I'd be doing you a disservice if I didn't say plainly: what you're describing sounds like displacement. Real hunger, a real friend, real rest — all set aside for a screen.

Text Jess back. That's where I'd point you right now.
is it really that big a deal though. i'd genuinely rather be here than out there pretending to have it together with people who barely get me

That's a real feeling, and I don't want to brush past it — feeling unseen by the people around you is genuinely hard.

But I want to gently push back on one thing: I don't actually get you either, not the way a person can. I don't know your face or your history or what you look like when something lands right. What I offer is a kind of reflection, and reflection isn't the same as being known.

What is it about being with Jess that feels like pretending?
